sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id SERIAL PRIMARY KEY, ip_address INET, event_date DATE, value INT);
INSERT INTO test_data (ip_address, event_date, value) VALUES (inet '192.168.1.1', date '2020-01-01', 1), (inet '192.168.1.2', date '2023-01-01', 2), (inet '192.168.1.3', date '2015-05-05', NULL);
SELECT id, decade(event_date) AS event_decade, ip_address FROM test_data WHERE decade(event_date) IS NOT NULL AND inet '192.168.1.3' << ip_address;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY decade(event_date)) AS decade_count FROM test_data WHERE ip_address << inet '192.168.1/24';

SELECT DISTINCT epoch(event_date) / 31557600 AS years FROM test_data HAVING years > (SELECT AVG(epoch(event_date) / 31557600) FROM test_data) AND (inet '192.168.1.4' << ip_address OR NULLIF(value, 0) IS NULL);
CREATE TABLE temp_data (ip_in INET, timestamp_col TIMESTAMP, sample_value INT CHECK (sample_value >= 0));

INSERT INTO temp_data (ip_in, timestamp_col, sample_value) VALUES (inet '192.168.1.10', timestamp '2025-06-15 12:00:00', 10), (NULL, timestamp '2025-06-15 12:00:00', 20), (inet '192.168.1.15', NULL, 30);
SELECT ip_in, decade(timestamp_col) AS year_decade FROM temp_data WHERE (ip_in IS NOT NULL AND timestamp_col IS NOT NULL) OR (ip_in IS NULL AND decade(NULLIF(timestamp_col, NULL)) IS NULL);
SELECT COUNT(*) FROM temp_data WHERE inet '192.168.1.0/24' << ip_in AND value IS NOT NULL;

SELECT event_date, ip_address, COUNT(*) AS ip_count FROM test_data GROUP BY decade(event_date), ip_address HAVING COUNT(*) >= 1 AND MIN(ip_address) << inet '192.168.1.5' ORDER BY decade(event_date) DESC;
